name: Blarify
description: builds a code knowledge graph (AST + LSP) into a graph DB for structural retrieval

developer-org.comment: Open-source graph builder maintained by Blar, the team behind the blar.io code-review platform.

first-release.comment: First PyPI release 0.1.2 in Nov 2024; latest 1.3.9 on 2025-10-20.

status.comment: Actively developed; latest commit on main dated 2026-05-25 (checked 2026-07-09).

website.comment: Homepage listed on PyPI; blar.io is the parent product page.

github-stars.comment: ~231 stars, 43 forks (checked 2026-07-09; GitHub cached figures ranged 199-231 during the check).

form-factor.comment: Ships as a Python library (GraphBuilder / Neo4jManager API) and an optional MCP server (pip install 'blarify[mcp]').

standalone-usable.comment: Usable from any Python project or added to any MCP-capable agent; not welded into a single host.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone OSS tool, not a bundled engine.

install-mechanism.comment: pip install blarify (or 'blarify[mcp]'); optional Docker to run a local Neo4j for the graph store.

retrieval-technique.comment: Parses code with tree-sitter (syntax) plus LSP/SCIP (semantic references) into a Neo4j/FalkorDB knowledge graph of files, classes, functions and CALLS/IMPORTS/INHERITS edges. No embeddings.

query-interface.comment: MCP tools (find_nodes_by_name_and_type, find_nodes_by_path, find_nodes_by_code, get_code_by_id, etc.); also queryable directly via graph DB (Cypher). find_nodes_by_code offers text-based lookup.

returns.comment: Returns node code (get_code_by_id / get_file_context_by_id), matching symbols, and relationship/workflow graphs (get_relationship_flowchart emits Mermaid diagrams).

structural-awareness.comment: Built on tree-sitter parsing and LSP; nodes are folders/files/classes/functions.

chunking-strategy.comment: Graph nodes map to files, classes and functions rather than fixed-size windows.

reranking.comment: No documented second-stage rerank; retrieval is graph traversal / structural lookup.

index-location.comment: Graph is stored in a Neo4j or FalkorDB instance the user runs (locally via Docker or self-hosted).

incremental-updates.comment: ProjectGraphDiffCreator detects changes and compares against previous node states (MODIFIED/DELETED tracking) rather than full rebuilds.

real-time-watch.comment: Re-indexing is invoked manually; no file-watcher/auto-sync. README lists graceful auto-update on file changes as future work.

vector-store.comment: Graph-based; no embeddings, so no vector store. Backing stores are graph DBs (Neo4j / FalkorDB).

embedding-model.comment: n-a — non-embedding, structural graph retrieval technique.

mcp-support.comment: Ships an MCP server exposing ~10 tools; installed via pip install 'blarify[mcp]'.

agent-compatibility.comment: Generic MCP server; docs give Claude Desktop config explicitly and reference other MCP-compatible assistants.

ide-integrations.comment: No dedicated IDE extension; surfaces through MCP clients (e.g. Claude Desktop) and direct library/DB use.

language-agnostic.comment: Retrieval quality depends on tree-sitter/LSP parser support; limited to the languages with parsers.

supported-languages.value: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Ruby, Go, C# (tree-sitter + LSP); Java and PHP via tree-sitter

code-leaves-machine.comment: The OSS graph builder runs locally against a self-hosted graph DB; no source or embeddings sent to a third party by default. (The separate blar.io hosted product is out of scope.)

local-only-option.comment: Fully offline path: local Neo4j/FalkorDB, tree-sitter/LSP parsing, no external API required (no embeddings).

self-hostable.comment: MIT OSS run on your own infrastructure with your own graph DB.

telemetry.comment: No telemetry/analytics documented in the repo (checked 2026-07-09).

max-repo-scale.comment: No explicit scale claim; batch node creation and SCIP support (up to 330x faster reference resolution) target performance on larger repos, but LSP-based graph building bounds very-large monorepo use. Judged medium.

pricing-model.comment: MIT open source, self-run; no cost for the tool itself. (The blar.io hosted platform is a separate product.)

maturity.comment: At v1.3.x with steady releases and active commits, but single-org and still evolving (auto-update, more language servers on the roadmap).

active-maintenance.comment: Latest commit on main dated 2026-05-25, within ~90 days of the 2026-07-09 check.
